title: Why did Gendo take Yuis last name
display_title: Why did Gendo take Yuis last name
wikitext:
[]: Why did Gendo take Yui's last name?             Quote Post by Aidan_Mclaren » Wed Mar 11, 2009 11:28 am As everyone knows, it is rare that a man would change his surname to that of his wife's. Does anyone know why Gendo would have allowed that?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 11, 2009 11:37 am She had a higher social status than him. Yui's father was some Seele bigwig; Gendo was basically a random nobody "marrying into" the organization. Getting rid of his original surname may have also been a way to symbolically cast away his past, which doesn't seem to have been the most pleasant one.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Mar 11, 2009 12:13 pm Add to that the fact that he doesn't seem to value himself so much from what I've deduced of his character...indeed, he states to Prof. Fuyutsuki after emerging from the police station that he isn't used to people being nice to him.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Wed Mar 11, 2009 2:18 pm He was the submissive in their relationship. Yui "wore the pants" so using her name was a way to mark Gendo as her own.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Mar 11, 2009 2:23 pm Anno's feminism probably had more than a little to do with it.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Mar 11, 2009 4:12 pm Because he loved her.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Wed Mar 11, 2009 4:14 pm Becuase who the fuck would want the last name Rokubundi (or however it's spelt.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 11, 2009 4:48 pm master_lloyd wrote: Because he loved her.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Mar 11, 2009 7:51 pm I always thought in Japan the husband takes the wife's name.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 11, 2009 8:13 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I always thought in Japan the husband takes the wife's name.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Wed Mar 11, 2009 10:46 pm Reichu wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: I always thought in Japan the husband takes the wife's name.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Mar 12, 2009 9:28 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: I always thought in Japan the husband takes the wife's name.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Thu Mar 12, 2009 9:39 am BobBQ wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: I always thought in Japan the husband takes the wife's name.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu Mar 12, 2009 9:47 am Reichu wrote: master_lloyd wrote: Because he loved her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Mar 12, 2009 9:49 am Reichu wrote: Yeah, you'll have to forgive me. That social psychology class is poisoning my mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Aidan_Mclaren » Thu Mar 12, 2009 8:02 pm Gendo doesn't strike me as the kind of person to be subservient to anyone. Unless Yui is an extremely dominant female. I agree with Reichu.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 12, 2009 8:15 pm MatrixRefugee wrote: :: Laughs:: Love that line, love the anime ("My Neighbors the Yamadas", that is).

[]:             Quote Post by LIGHTFIRELT » Sat Mar 14, 2009 4:35 pm Didn't they make this pretty clear in the show? Unless you didn't know that custom.

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Sat Mar 14, 2009 10:58 pm I've been told a lot of Asian cultures have a family registry that's pretty important. Korean dude was telling me that because of low birth rates, if you don't have a male heir, or adopt one, the government closes your registry and it's almost like your family never existed. Don't know how much of that is applicable here, but that would give a precedent to men taking their wive's surname for reasons of continuing their family line. Also given the circumstances of their marriage being shortly after the greatest human die off ever, such things were probably much more common. Plus any personal reasons Gendo may have had.

[]: Well...             Quote Post by TequilaBard » Thu Apr 23, 2009 3:50 am Are we basing this idea off of the Gendo we know in the series, or the 'Bastard King', utterly emotionless bastard the fandom has turned him into? The Gendo in the series was so devoted to the concept of reuniting with his wife that he wanted to off humanity. Now, maybe I'm a little skewed here, but taking his wife's name sounds in line with a person that devoted to a woman. Hell, Yui and Fuyutsuki seem to be the only people that cared for him, and Fuyutsuki much more in a 'well, he's got in another situation I gotta bail his ass out of...'
